title: poa ligularis var. resinulosa
intro: Poa ligularis var. resinulosa, also known as resinous blue grass, is a type of grass native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its resinous stem has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The resinulosa varietal of Poa ligularis is said to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Poa ligularis var. resinulosa include infusions, tinctures, and ointments that can be applied topically or taken internally, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
